mnemonics.title: Function Parts Remember
mnemonics.content: Remember function structure as: Return Type + Function Name + Parameters + Body. Think: 'Richard Needs Pretty Underwear Daily' (Return Name Parameters Underwear Daily)

mnemonics.title: Cast Types记忆
mnemonics.content: SDCR for C++ casts: S-tatic, D-ynamic, C-onst, R-einterpret. Or remember: 'Some Dumb Compiler Rules' for compile-time vs runtime casting.

mnemonics.title: Pass by Reference vs Value
mnemonics.content: PASS BY VALUE: Copy created (like photocopy). PASS BY REFERENCE: Original document (like sharing the original file). Changes to copy don't affect original; changes to original do.

acronyms.usage: Model for functions: Accept input, process it, produce output
Describes how function call stack works in recursion
Principle that justifies using functions for code reuse

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a stack of plates. Each function call adds a plate (stack frame) on top. When function returns, plate is removed. Recursive functions keep adding/removing plates until base condition plate is reached.
Imagine type casting like water containers. Small container (int) to big container (double) pours automatically (implicit). Big to small needs explicit pour to avoid overflow (explicit cast).
Think of it like giving someone your house key (reference) vs giving them a photocopy of your house (value). With key, they can change your house. With photocopy, your original stays safe.

analogies.analogy: Like a recipe in a cookbook. The recipe (function) tells you steps to make a dish. You can use the same recipe many times (reusability) without rewriting instructions each time.
Like a movie trailer. It announces what's coming (function name, return type, parameters) before the full movie (function definition) is shown.
Like converting currency at a bank. Sometimes it's automatic (USD to EUR at good rate = implicit), sometimes you need to explicitly request conversion (explicit cast).
